suggestion: The topic is reasonably strong for a top ML conference: it is specific to a concrete mechanism (adaptive multi-reference self-attention in a world-model/FreeCustom setting), targets a clear problem (multi-concept composition), and appears implementable with standard ablations. Novelty is moderate rather than high because dynamic attention reweighting by similarity is a natural extension of existing attention/control methods. To strengthen it, narrow the technical claim and evaluation axis, e.g.: "Adaptive concept-similarity-gated multi-reference self-attention for training-free multi-concept personalization in diffusion world models, with explicit token-level routing and compositional consistency benchmarks."
